question_text: A student drops a marble and a bowling ball from the same height at the same time in a vacuum chamber. Which statement best explains what happens?

options.label: A
options.text: The marble falls faster because it is lighter and experiences less air resistance.

options.label: B
options.text: The bowling ball falls faster because it has more mass and experiences stronger gravity.

options.label: C
options.text: Both objects fall at the same rate because gravity accelerates all objects equally in a vacuum.

options.label: D
options.text: They fall at different rates because the bowling ball creates a stronger gravitational field.

explanation: In a vacuum, there is no air resistance, so all objects accelerate downwards at the same rate (10 m/s² on Earth) regardless of their mass. This is because although heavier objects experience stronger gravitational force, they also have more inertia, which perfectly balances out.
